summary.intent: Install a routing package for the Expo app and create file-based routes for each screen, plus add a new CheckpointTimeline screen.
summary.outcome: Migrated to expo-router with file-based routing in a `routes/` directory, updated all screens to use useRouter/useLocalSearchParams, and created a new CheckpointTimeline screen with a vertical timeline UI.

repo: Project lives at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app/ — an Expo React Native app with screens in src/screens/ and API logic in src/api.ts
Screens: ActiveSessions, SessionDetail, SessionTree, Checkpoints, CheckpointDetail — all previously used navigation props instead of hooks
The app uses WebSockets for live updates (used in CheckpointTimeline)
There is a pre-existing non-fatal checkpoint worktree warning on the API server at localhost:3000
expo-router root directory can be configured via app.json plugin options — needed to avoid the app/app/ double-nesting collision

code.finding: expo-router plugin accepts a root option to set the routes directory name, avoiding collision with the project's own app/ folder
expo-router requires main field set to 'expo-router/entry' to take over as the entry point
Root Stack layout defined here; each screen embeds its own Stack.Screen options for title and header buttons
New screen: vertical timeline rail with colored dots per branch name, sorted oldest-to-newest by checkpoint id, live WebSocket updates

workflow: expo-router uses file-based routing similar to Next.js — dynamic segments use [param].tsx syntax
When an Expo project folder is named 'app/', expo-router's default 'app/' routes directory creates an awkward app/app/ nesting — always configure a custom root (e.g. 'routes') upfront
Metro bundler hot-reloads on directory renames without requiring a server restart

friction: Naming collision: expo-router defaults to an 'app/' routes directory, but the Expo project itself is already in an 'app/' folder, resulting in app/app/ — required a post-hoc rename and app.json fix
Required a second pass to update package.json main field after initial edit was incomplete
open_items: Verify expo-router deep-link scheme ('gossamer') works correctly on iOS/Android for navigation between screens
The checkpoint worktree warning on the API server is pre-existing and was not investigated or resolved
No navigation tests or snapshot tests were added for the new routing structure
CheckpointTimeline branch color assignment is based on branch name hashing — exact color palette/accessibility not reviewed
